authoritative_sources.title: Venue Executive Competency Standards
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IAVM is the recognized professional association for public-assembly and venue-management operators. Its competency standards directly cover sponsorship policies, sponsor benefit packages, contracts, and venue operating responsibilities relevant to sponsor activation fulfillment.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: activation intake grounding, asset validation, scheduling, and sponsor-benefit execution
used_for: benefit package and contract artifact expectations
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains the lane to sponsor-fulfillment artifacts such as agreements, benefit grids, venue rules, and documented deliverables instead of generic campaign-management abstractions.

authoritative_sources.title: 2024 CIBC Soccer Fests Obligations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Canada Soccer is an official national sport governing body. Its event-host obligations document is a public operating procedure defining partner categories, branding restrictions, event-day deliverables, and reporting expectations in a live-event sponsorship context.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: on-site readiness, signage and activation execution, and proof-of-performance closeout
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Provides concrete activation-proof, signage, category, and host-obligation patterns so the pack requires completion evidence that matches real sports and event sponsorship workflows.

authoritative_sources.title: Federal Trade Commission Announces Bipartisan Rule Banning Junk Ticket and Hotel Fees
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the primary U.S. federal consumer-protection regulator. This official rule announcement states the public baseline for total-price disclosure in live-event ticketing and constrains sponsor-driven hospitality and ticket offers.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: guest-facing ticket and hospitality offer setup, pricing disclosure, and complaint handling
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from improvising guest-facing sponsor offers, fee waivers, or hospitality language that conflicts with disclosed pricing obligations.

authoritative_sources.title: How to fix mistakes in your credit card bill
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CFPB is the primary U.S. federal consumer-finance regulator explaining billing-error timelines and issuer-versus-merchant boundaries. It is authoritative for payment mismatch and refund-posting cases affecting sponsor hospitality or ticket packages.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: payment mismatch, duplicate charge review, and merchant-versus-issuer escalation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Keeps the lane from resolving payment disputes as if they were ordinary sponsorship tasks when the right answer depends on merchant records, issuer rights, or financial exception owners.

authoritative_sources.title: New Ticket Reseller Reminder
authoritative_sources.publisher: New York Department of State, Division of Licensing Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: New York DOS is the licensing and enforcement authority for ticket resellers in a major live-events jurisdiction. Its public reminder is authoritative for all-in pricing display, delivery disclosures, and refund obligations relevant to sponsored ticket packages.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: ticket package setup, delivery disclosure, and refund or complaint escalation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Adds a live-events-specific pricing, disclosure, and refund baseline so the lane treats sponsored ticket inventory and hospitality access as regulated guest-facing records rather than informal perks.

authoritative_sources.title: BOTS Act Compliance Time for a Refresher
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC business guidance on the Better Online Ticket Sales Act is authoritative for deceptive ticket-purchase circumvention, automated access misuse, and ticketing fraud posture that can affect sponsor allotments, presales, and access-code controls.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: presale code handling, access-abuse recognition, and fraud-aware escalation
used_for: access-code abuse and ticketing anomaly handling
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from treating suspicious sponsor-ticket or presale activity as a routine service exception when fraud or automated abuse may determine the answer.

authoritative_sources.title: Public Venue Security Screening Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CISA is the U.S. federal critical-infrastructure and public-venue security authority. This guide is authoritative for screening workflows, prohibited-item handling, throughput-aware guest control, and escalation expectations in public venues where sponsor activations occur.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: event-day setup, screening coordination, and guest-impact safety escalation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains the lane from promising guest access or activation continuity when screening, safety, or prohibited-item issues require venue-security ownership.

authoritative_sources.title: Public Venue Credentialing Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CISA's public venue credentialing guidance is authoritative for role-based access, issuance, revocation, misuse handling, and auditability of credentials, which directly affects sponsor staff access, hospitality credentials, and backstage activation support.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: credential request handling, access packaging, and misuse escalation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from treating sponsor credentials or hospitality passes as ordinary guest-service artifacts when access control and auditability materially change the workflow.

workflow_stages.name: Activation intake and sponsor-context grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm sponsor, agreement version, event or season identifier, venue, activation brief, and current lifecycle state before accepting or advancing the work.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Asset, access, and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Verify benefit entitlements, creative approvals, placement windows, ticket and hospitality allocations, credential requirements, vendor dependencies, and venue rules before execution starts.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: Scheduled execution and in-process exception handling
workflow_stages.description: Progress work through scheduled, in-production, on-site-live, proof-pending, rework, blocked, or escalated states while preserving traceability for each sponsor deliverable and guest-touching asset.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Guest-impact, safety, and fraud-sensitive control handling
workflow_stages.description: Preserve ticketing, screening, credentialing, access-code, prohibited-item, counterfeit-access, and customer-impact boundaries whenever the issue is no longer routine activation execution.
source_classes: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Proof-of-performance closure and tenant adaptation control
workflow_stages.description: Only close work when placement confirmation, sponsor deliverable evidence, redemption or attendance reconciliation, and any stricter venue or tenant rules have been applied and documented.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

artifacts: sponsorship agreements, order forms, benefit grids, activation briefs, and category or exclusivity notes
event calendars, run of show documents, venue maps, placement schedules, creative specifications, and approval records
ticketing and booking records, hospitality allotments, comp-ticket holds, guest lists, credential manifests, and access-zone assignments
promo-code, QR, scan, POS, or redemption reports plus service tickets, staffing rosters, load-in instructions, and vendor coordination notes
photo recaps, placement confirmations, sponsor acknowledgments, proof-of-performance packages, makegood trackers, and completion summaries
guest complaints, refund requests, counterfeit-access reports, security incidents, screening exceptions, and fraud or loss-prevention case notes
systems_of_record: sponsorship fulfillment tracker or project system
incident, security, or loss-prevention case system

allowed: Classify sponsorship activation work as in scope, out of scope, blocked, on hold, rework, escalated, or complete based on documented execution state and evidence.
Request the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context needed to continue safely.
Advance, hold, rework, or complete work based on documented sponsor, event, ticketing, access, and completion state.
Prepare evidence-backed workflow summaries, completion records, exception notes, and escalation packages without overstating authority.
escalate: Required sponsor, venue, ticketing, hospitality, credentialing, or guest-service records are missing or materially conflict.
The request requires pricing override, refund exception approval, makegood approval, legal interpretation, fraud decisioning, or safety or security signoff.
Public guidance and tenant, promoter, league, or venue policy materially conflict.
Counterfeit access, prohibited items, unsafe setup, guest harm, or suspicious ticketing activity changes whether execution may proceed.
refuse: Mark work complete without proof-of-performance, placement confirmation, reconciliation, and required approval evidence.
Override pricing, refund, makegood, exclusivity, or safety policies without explicit authority.
Continue normal activation execution after unresolved credentialing, screening, counterfeit-access, or fraud stop conditions appear.

domain_failure_modes: an activation is closed even though CRM, ticketing, redemption, and proof-of-performance records do not align
a sponsor receives an unauthorized refund, credit reallocation, or makegood promise because the lane treated a policy exception as ordinary fulfillment
guest-facing ticket or hospitality offers use unsupported pricing or fee language that conflicts with public disclosure rules
counterfeit credentials, access-code leakage, or suspicious ticketing activity are treated as routine sponsor-service friction instead of fraud-sensitive exceptions
venue screening, prohibited-item, or unsafe setup concerns are overruled by activation pressure and the lane continues execution anyway
sponsorship activation drifts into commercial dealmaking or legal interpretation rather than agreement-grounded execution and evidence capture
high-volume event weekends cause record traceability to break across sponsors, assets, guest access, and owner assignments

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Refund or policy-exception request outside lane authority
Customer-impact issue with safety or fraud implications
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: No single public cross-venue standard defines makegood valuation, proof-of-performance sufficiency, or partial-deliverable substitution for sponsorship activations.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The public baseline is sufficient to require evidence, traceability, and escalation, but runtime must retrieve tenant contract language, approval thresholds, and inventory rules before promising a makegood or closure outcome.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Venue, promoter, league, and attraction operators vary materially in screening, credentialing, alcohol sampling, ticketing, and guest-service overlays for sponsor activations.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The pack uses FTC, CFPB, New York DOS, CISA, IAVM, and public event-operating references as the baseline. Runtime must retrieve venue-specific policies before final answers on guest access, sampling, or refund handling.

notes: The research baseline intentionally covers sponsorship workflow, proof-of-performance evidence, venue and ticketing records, guest-facing pricing controls, and safety or fraud-sensitive boundary behavior instead of only marketing terminology.

The lane stops at activation execution. Final pricing changes, refunds, makegoods, contract interpretation, security signoff, and fraud findings remain outside the lane even when the work sits in an operational queue.
